zh.lifeSummary: 當Matilda of England, Duchess of Saxony於1153年6月出生在英國波克夏溫莎女爵 卡素時，她的父親Henry II King of England20歲，母親Aliénor d'Aquitaine Reine de France puis Reine d'Angleterre30歲。她於1168年2月1日與Henry "The Lion" Duke of Saxony and Bavaria在德國諾爾德海-維斯特法倫可萊斯 明登-盧貝可明登結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1189年6月28日在德國尼德薩辛布勞恩史維布勞恩史維享年36歲，埋葬在德國布勞恩史維布勞恩史維。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Matilda of England, Duchess of Saxony于1153年6月出生在英国波克夏溫莎女爵 卡素时，她的父亲Henry II King of England20岁，母亲Aliénor d'Aquitaine Reine de France puis Reine d'Angleterre30岁。她于1168年2月1日与Henry "The Lion" Duke of Saxony and Bavaria在德国諾爾德海-維斯特法倫可萊斯 明登-盧貝可明登结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1189年6月28日在德国尼德薩辛布勞恩史維布勞恩史維享年36岁，埋葬在德国布勞恩史維布勞恩史維。

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Cheng, Jing, Kam Fung, Wai Shing, Wing, Yali, Yuk.
variant Romanization of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Ou
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ying, Ching, Heng, Hong, Jia, Kuang Yu, Li-Ching, Cho, Chong, Chung, Sa.  Southeast Asian Lichun, Ming, Quan, Roeun, Sokhom, Chau, Hai, Leang, Ly, Nhanh, Phan, Pho, Tha.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 歐, meaning ‘to sing someone's praise’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Dong Ou (東甌), a fief (in present-day Wenzhou in Zhejiang province) which was granted to Yao in 192  BC . It is said that Yao was a descendant of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the placename Ou Yu (歐餘), the name of a fief (located in present-day Zhejiang province) that was granted to Ti, the second son of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 區, originally denoting an ancient vessel for measurement in ancient Chinese: from the personal name of Ou Zhizi (歐治子), an expert sword-maker who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). Some of his descendants inherited Ou (歐) as their surname, simplifying the Chinese character 歐 to 區.

nameMeanings.name: England,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Middle English Engelond ‘England’. It was probably a formal alternative to English , which is also well evidenced as a surname in England. These names may have been acquired by English landowners who moved in Norman social circles or who lived in a neighbouring country (Scotland, Wales, or Ireland), or by English merchants who traded abroad.
Norwegian: habitational name from any of various farmsteads so named, from Old Norse eng ‘meadow’ + land ‘land’.
Swedish: ornamental name with the same meaning as 2.

variants.name: English
meanings: English, Scottish, and Welsh: ethnic name from Middle English English, Inglish, Inglis ‘English’ (Old English Englisc), sometimes alternating with Anglo-Norman French Engleis, Engles, Anglais, Angles (Old French Englois). Compare Inglis and England . Among the aristocracy and upper gentry it marked out a man of English ancestry from one of Norman or continental origin. In counties bordering England with Scotland and Wales the name distinguished an Englishman from a Scot or a native Welshman on both sides of the border. The name may also have been acquired by English merchants who traded abroad or who lived and worked in a ‘French’ borough in England (one exclusively administered by Normans).
Irish: in Ireland, this name was used to denote an Englishman, often being adopted for Irish Aingléis ‘Englishman’ or through mistranslation for Mac an Ghallóglaigh, see Gallogly and Golightly .

nameMeanings.name: Matilda
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

variants.name: Mathilda
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

en.lifeSummary: When Matilda of England, Duchess of Saxony was born in June 1153, in Windsor Castle, Berkshire, England, her father, Henry II King of England, was 20 and her mother, Aliénor d'Aquitaine Reine de France puis Reine d'Angleterre, was 30. She married Henry "The Lion" Duke of Saxony and Bavaria on 1 February 1168, in Minden, Minden-Lübbecke, North Rhine-Westphalia, Germany. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. She died on 28 June 1189, in Braunschweig, Brunswick, Lower Saxony, Germany, at the age of 36, and was buried in Brunswick, Brunswick, Germany.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Matilda of England, Duchess of Saxony est née en juin 1153, à Windsor Castle, Berkshire, Angleterre, son père, Henry II King of England, avait 20 ans et sa mère, Aliénor d'Aquitaine Reine de France puis Reine d'Angleterre, avait 30 ans. Elle a épousé Henry "The Lion" Duke of Saxony and Bavaria le 1 février 1168, à Minden, Kreis Minden-Lübbecke, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Allemagne. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 28 juin 1189, à Braunschweig, Braunschweig, Niedersachsen, Allemagne, à l’âge de 36 ans, et a été enterrée à Braunschweig, Braunschweig, Allemagne.

de.lifeSummary: Als Matilda of England, Duchess of Saxony im Juni 1153 in Windsor Castle, Berkshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Henry II King of England 20 und ihre Mutter Aliénor d'Aquitaine Reine de France puis Reine d'Angleterre 30. Sie hat am 1. Februar 1168 in Minden, Kreis Minden-Lübbecke, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Deutschland Henry "The Lion" Duke of Saxony and Bavaria geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb am 28. Juni 1189 im Alter von 36 Jahren in Braunschweig, Braunschweig, Niedersachsen, Deutschland und wurde in Braunschweig, Braunschweig, Deutschland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Matilda of England, Duchess of Saxony è nata il nel giugno 1153 nel Windsor Castle, Berkshire, England, sua padre, Henry II King of England, aveva 20 anni e sua madre, Aliénor d'Aquitaine Reine de France puis Reine d'Angleterre, aveva 30 anni. Lei sposato Henry "The Lion" Duke of Saxony and Bavaria il 1 febbraio 1168, nel Minden, Kreis Minden-Lübbecke, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Germania. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 28 giugno 1189, nel Braunschweig, Braunschweig, Niedersachsen, Germania, all'età di 36 anni, e fu sepolta a Braunschweig, Braunschweig, Germania.

ja.lifeSummary: Matilda of England, Duchess of Saxonyが1153年6月にイギリスバークシュアイアウィンドソル カストルで生まれた時，父Henry II King of Englandは20歳，母Aliénor d'Aquitaine Reine de France puis Reine d'Angleterreは30歳であった。1168年2月1日にドイツノードルヘイン-ウエストファレンクレイス ミンデン-ルuベクミンデンでHenry "The Lion" Duke of Saxony and Bavariaと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1189年6月28日にドイツニーダーサチセンブラウンスチウエイグブラウンスチウエイグで36歳で死去し，ドイツブラウンスチウエイグブラウンスチウエイグにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Matilda of England, Duchess of Saxony(이)가 1153년 6월 영국 버크실르 윈드소르 카스틀에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Henry II King of England(은)는 20세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Aliénor d'Aquitaine Reine de France puis Reine d'Angleterre(은)는 30세였다. 그녀는 1168년 2월 1일 독일 노르드하이-베스트파렌 크레이스 민데-u브벡 민든에서 Henry "The Lion" Duke of Saxony and Bavaria(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1189년 6월 28일 독일 니더작센 브라슈위그 브라슈위그에서 36세의 나이로 사망했으며 독일 브라슈위그 브라슈위그에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Matilda of England, Duchess of Saxony nasceu em junho de 1153, em Windsor Castle, Berkshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Henry II King of England, tinha 20 anos e sua mãe, Aliénor d'Aquitaine Reine de France puis Reine d'Angleterre, tinha 30 anos. Ela casou-se com Henry "The Lion" Duke of Saxony and Bavaria em 1 de fevereiro de 1168, em Minden, Kreis Minden-Lübbecke, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Alemanha. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 28 de junho de 1189, em Braunschweig, Braunschweig, Niedersachsen, Alemanha, com 36 anos, e foi sepultada em Braunschweig, Braunschweig, Alemanha.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Matilda of England, Duchess of Saxony родилась июнь 1153 г. в местности виндзор кастл, биркшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Henry II King of England было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Aliénor d'Aquitaine Reine de France puis Reine d'Angleterre, 30. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Henry "The Lion" Duke of Saxony and Bavaria 1 февраля 1168 г. в местности минден, крис минден-лuббекк, нордраин-вестфеилен, Германия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла 28 июня 1189 г. в местности браунщвиг, браунщвиг, ниедирсачсен, Германия в возрасте 36 лет и была похоронена в местности браунщвиг, браунщвиг, Германия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Matilda of England, Duchess of Saxony nació en junio de 1153, en Windsor Castle, Berkshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Henry II King of England, tenía 20 años y su mamá, Aliénor d'Aquitaine Reine de France puis Reine d'Angleterre, tenía 30 años. Se casó con Henry "The Lion" Duke of Saxony and Bavaria el 1 de febrero de 1168, en Minden, Kreis Minden-Lübbecke, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Alemania. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 28 de junio de 1189, en Braunschweig, Braunschweig, Niedersachsen, Alemania, a la edad de 36 años, y fue sepultada en Braunschweig, Braunschweig, Alemania.
